verdict: Good app, but not top-tier yet
verdict_explanation: Tests are green (118 passed). Production code is functionally solid and cleanly separated from _common. The primary structural drag is a test helper with duplicated stdlib imports generating pervasive lint noise across 7 test files. run_review() at 405 LoC is secondary debt. Neither is a disqualifier, but both suppress the score below contest-grade.

architecture_quality.proof: run_review.py:339 (PROVIDERS[reviewer]['build_cmd'](build_args, session_id) — registry dispatch); run_review.py:25-69 (clean import DAG: run_review -> _common.*); no costume layers identified in full source walk

state_management.proof: run_review.py:187 (_active_proc module-level, single writer at :375 and :598); run_review.py:209 (resume_requested snapshot prevents arg mutation); run_review.py:451-453 (local state machine use_resume/fallback_used/session_id)

domain_modeling.proof: run_review.py:73 (REVIEWER_ALIASES dict); run_review.py:121-122 (normalization at CLI boundary); PROVIDERS registry from _common is the core model

data_flow.proof: run_review.py:27-69 (clean DAG: all imports from _common or stdlib; no cycles); run_review.py:501-510 (temporal coupling documented: pre-read output_content before extract_text_from_output overwrites file)

framework_idioms.proof: scripts/tests/_helpers.py:12-22 (9 unused stdlib imports — F401 per ruff); run_review.py:605 (PTH108: os.unlink instead of Path.unlink); run_review.py:27 (I001: unsorted imports)

concurrency.proof: run_review.py:322-325 (signal.getsignal saved; signal.signal restores in finally at :598-602); run_review.py:192 (_signal_handler reads _active_proc, calls _kill_tree); run_review.py:601-605 (gemini/agy cleanup in finally)

simplicity.proof: run_review.py:201-605 (405 LoC, 67 AST nested control-flow nodes per ast.walk analysis); scripts/tests/_helpers.py:12-22 (dual-path stdlib imports add confusion)

test_strategy.proof: 118 tests green; run_review.py:413 guard tested by test_run_review_codex_stale_events_do_not_mask_empty_current_output (rc==124); Gemini overlay tested at test_execution_paths.py:138-214; gap: no sync test between parse_args() and make_args()

credibility.proof: Production code earns its architecture at the _common boundary; test infrastructure carries two drift risks: _helpers.py unused-import accumulation (F401 noise across 7 files); manually-maintained make_args() dict that drifted in loop 1

strengths: run_review.py:339 — provider dispatch is fully registry-driven (PROVIDERS[reviewer]['build_cmd']); orchestrator never branches on provider internals
Gemini config-overlay isolation tested behaviorally: temp dir excludes auto-saved policy files (test_execution_paths.py:178-214); settings preserved (test_execution_paths.py:138-175)
Resume/fallback state tracked via locals (use_resume, fallback_used at run_review.py:210-211); args never mutated (snapshot at :209)

findings.title: Test helper re-exports stdlib it does not use, generating pervasive lint noise
findings.why_it_matters: The star-import pattern forces all 7 test files to suppress ruff lint via noqa annotations, disabling F401/F405 detection that would catch future undefined-name errors.
findings.what_is_wrong: scripts/tests/_helpers.py:12-22 imports 9 stdlib modules (json, os, shutil, signal, stat, subprocess, sys, tempfile, unittest/mock) that _helpers.py does not itself use. Test files already import these directly on their own line 2. Removing them from _helpers.py breaks nothing and eliminates pervasive lint suppression.
evidence: scripts/tests/_helpers.py:12-22 (F401 unused imports: json, os, shutil, signal, stat, subprocess, sys, tempfile, unittest, mock)
scripts/tests/test_execution_paths.py:2 (imports same stdlib directly — duplicate path)
ruff F405 at test_execution_paths.py:18,31,39 (self_check may be undefined or from star imports)
ruff F405 at test_execution_paths.py:47,52,57 (run_script may be undefined or from star imports)
findings.leverage_impact: Zero: each test file already imports stdlib directly; star re-export provides no new capability.
findings.locality_impact: Negative: F401 noise in _helpers.py + noqa suppressions in 7 test files degrade ruff signal across the entire test directory.
findings.metric_signal: ruff exit code 1; 9 F401 violations in _helpers.py; 3 F405 violations in test_execution_paths.py
findings.why_weakens_submission: A test helper generating structural lint noise across 7 files and suppressing undefined-name detection is a credibility deduction; it signals accumulated drift and prevents tooling from flagging future issues.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Remove unused stdlib imports from scripts/tests/_helpers.py:12-22 (json, os, shutil, signal, stat, subprocess, sys, tempfile, unittest, mock). Keep: import argparse (used at :85), from run_review import ... (used at :32-34), from _common.* imports (used at :35-37). Each test file's direct imports on line 2 already cover stdlib needs.

findings.title: make_args() has no contract-sync test — will drift again on next arg addition
findings.why_it_matters: Loop 1 was entirely consumed by make_args() drifting from parse_args(). The fix was one dict entry; the structural cause is unchanged. Next parse_args() addition will silently break codex tests again.
findings.what_is_wrong: scripts/tests/_helpers.py:64-85 builds a Namespace by hand from a hard-coded dict. No test asserts that make_args().keys() == parse_args([]).keys(). Authority Map: Split and ambiguous — two sources of truth with no mechanical enforcement.
evidence: scripts/tests/_helpers.py:64-85 (manual dict — 16 keys today; parse_args/make_args key sets verified to match via Python introspection but no automated enforcement)
Loop 1 finding F-001 resolved by one-line dict patch with no structural prevention
findings.leverage_impact: Low: make_args() proxies parse_args() with no added behavior; a parse_args()-derived version would be identical at call sites.
findings.locality_impact: Every new CLI argument requires two edits: run_review.py:parse_args() + _helpers.py:make_args(). The second is easy to miss.
findings.metric_signal: none (drift is invisible until a test fails)
findings.why_weakens_submission: A helper that drifted once and has no mechanical prevention is a credibility risk; the backlog carries the fix rather than the certitude.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Add a test (~5 lines) in scripts/tests/test_file_io_validation.py: patch sys.argv to ['run_review.py'], call run_review.parse_args(), assert set(vars(args).keys()) == set(vars(make_args()).keys()). This prevents future drift without changing make_args() itself.

findings.title: PTH108 + I001 lint violations in run_review.py (pre-existing)
findings.why_it_matters: ruff check exits 1 on production code; PTH108 and I001 are auto-fixable but unaddressed, reducing credibility of the production module.
findings.what_is_wrong: run_review.py:605: os.unlink(agy_log_path) should be Path(agy_log_path).unlink() per PTH108. run_review.py:27: import block is unsorted per I001. run_review.py:36: noqa F401 is now unused (RUF100).
findings.locality_impact: Minor: RUF100 at :36 makes re-export intent invisible to tooling.
findings.metric_signal: ruff exit code 1; 3 violations in run_review.py
findings.why_weakens_submission: Pre-existing lint violations in the production module reduce credibility.
findings.minimal_correction_path: run_review.py:605: replace os.unlink(agy_log_path) with Path(agy_log_path).unlink(). Run ruff --fix scripts/run_review.py for I001 auto-fix. For RUF100 at :36: remove the # noqa: F401.

simplification_check.structurally_necessary: Removing unused imports from _helpers.py passes deletion test: zero behavior change since test files import stdlib directly. Adding parse_args/make_args sync test passes SPT Q1+Q2.
simplification_check.helpful_simplification: Removing stdlib re-export from _helpers.py simplifies lint picture across 7 files without adding dependency or ceremony.
simplification_check.should_not_be_done: Do not replace make_args() dict with parse_args() invocation yet (deepening candidate for a later loop). Do not add __all__ to _helpers.py as a costume-layer fix.
simplification_check.tests_after_fix: No tests deleted. Existing 118 tests continue to pass after _helpers.py cleanup. New sync test added for F2.

backlog.title: Add parse_args/make_args contract-sync test
backlog.why_it_matters: Prevents loop-1-class drift recurrence; makes arg-contract Authority Map verdict Single and clear.

backlog.title: Fix PTH108 + I001 in run_review.py
backlog.why_it_matters: Cleans production lint output; makes ruff check exit 0 on production code.

deepening_candidates.source_friction_proven: Finding F2 — make_args() manually copies arg names from parse_args() and drifted in loop 1
deepening_candidates.why_shallow_or_misplaced: make_args() provides no behavior beyond argparse defaults; equivalent to calling parse_args([]) with overrides
deepening_candidates.behavior_to_move_behind_interface: arg-name set derivation; default-value derivation from argparse
deepening_candidates.test_surface_after_change: same test files; make_args() call sites unchanged in signature
deepening_candidates.smallest_first_step: def make_args(**overrides): with mock.patch('sys.argv', ['run_review.py']): args = run_review.parse_args(); vars(args).update(overrides); return args
deepening_candidates.what_not_to_do: Do not add a new seam or ArgFactory class; do not change test call sites; execute only after F2 sync test is in place

builder_notes.pattern: Bulk stdlib re-export via star import as shared test namespace
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: grep _helpers.py for imports X where X appears in ruff F401 output AND appears in line 2 of every test file — both channels exist, star re-export is redundant
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: A helper module should import only what it needs. Export only shared symbols (functions, constants, fixtures) — not stdlib pass-throughs.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: _helpers.py should define make_args, run_script, SCRIPT_DIR, FIXTURES_DIR, _CREATE_NEW_PROCESS_GROUP only. Each test file imports stdlib directly (line 2 already does this).

builder_notes.pattern: Manually-maintained arg contract mirror in test helper
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: grep test helpers for argparse.Namespace(**{...}) or dict-based Namespace construction — each is a drift bomb when parse_args() adds new args
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Derive the Namespace from parse_args([]) with a controlled arg list, then override fields. One call; can't drift.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: def make_args(**overrides): with mock.patch('sys.argv', ['run_review.py']): args = run_review.parse_args(); vars(args).update(overrides); return args

builder_notes.how_to_recognize: Three borderline claims: (1) concurrency:8 — uncertain about _active_proc thread-safety under hypothetical multithreading not present in current usage; (2) framework_idioms:6 — uncertain whether star-export pattern is documented-intentional vs accumulated drift; (3) test_strategy:7 — may have missed 1-2 additional Authority Map gaps
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Surface uncertainty in builder notes rather than inflating the score.

narrative: Loop 2 is the first real structural critic pass. Production code earns 7-8 across most dimensions: the _common seam is honest, provider dispatch is registry-driven, signal handling is idiomatic. The drag is in test infrastructure: _helpers.py re-exports stdlib it doesn't use, generating F401/F405 lint noise across all 7 test files. The directly corrective fix is purely subtractive. The secondary risk is make_args() manual dict — loop 1 was entirely consumed by one drift instance; without a sync test, the same failure class recurs. Neither issue is a disqualifier but both suppress scores below contest-grade.

loop_result.change_summary: Removed 8 unused stdlib imports from scripts/tests/_helpers.py:11-22 (json, os, shutil, signal, stat, tempfile, unittest, from unittest import mock). Added noqa E402,F401 comments to intentional star-export anchors (lines 24-26, 83-86). Applied ruff --fix for I001 import sort. After change: ruff check on _helpers.py = 0 errors; full test suite = 118 passed, 0 failed.
loop_result.lint_result_after: 0 errors in _helpers.py; 3 pre-existing errors in run_review.py (F-004, not targeted this loop)

implementation_review.reason: The 8 unused stdlib imports cited in F-002 are genuinely absent from the current _helpers.py, the fix is a pure deletion with no redistribution, and the noqa annotations added are on intentional re-export anchors (not suppression of the structural smell).
